description: What: End-to-end agent loop. Why: The loop is the product. Contract: Each stage observable via RunActivity. Verification: Run agent; see stages emitted.

title: Implement transactional snapshot sealing
description: What: Seal atomically with persistence. Why: Partial seals corrupt the evidence plane. Contract: Seal either commits all refs or none. Verification: Crash during seal does not leave a partial row.

title: Implement dynamic watchlist derivation (screen/agent/theme/portfolio)
description: What: Four derivation modes with replayable definitions. Why: Frozen row caches drift. Contract: Refresh regenerates membership from the source definition. Verification: Modify screen; derived watchlist updates on refresh.

title: Implement IFRS metric mapping (canonical_source_class='ifrs')
description: What: Map IFRS statement lines to Metrics marked with canonical_source_class='ifrs'. Why: IFRS ≠ GAAP; must not silently merge. Contract: IFRS-sourced Facts carry the class distinction. Verification: IFRS-only issuer produces Facts with ifrs class.

title: Implement create_agent approval flow (pending_action)
description: What: create_agent tool returns pending_action_id. Why: Side-effect requires approval. Contract: Agent not enabled until approved. Verification: Analyst call creates pending; user approves; agent enabled.

title: Epic P4 — Product parity layers
description:
# Epic P4 — Product parity layers

## Goal

Close the gap between "working research product" and "feels like the videos": themes as first-class subjects, Analyze as a saved template workflow, the shared artifact flow ("Add results to chat"), the findings-first Home feed with cluster dedupe, the right-rail activity stream, specialized Reddit/news blocks, and dynamic watchlists / portfolio overlays.

## Child tasks

- P4.1 Themes & macro subjects (Theme, ThemeMembership, theme chats)
- P4.2 Analyze template system (CRUD + `analyze_template_run` bundle + memo layout)
- P4.3 Shared artifact flow (add Analyze memo into chat thread with shared snapshot)
- P4.4 Home feed ranking & cluster dedupe (cross-agent findings)
- P4.5 Right-rail activity stream (`Reading` / `Investigating` / `Found` / `Dismissed`)
- P4.6 Specialized social & news blocks (`news_cluster`, `sentiment_trend`, `mention_volume`)
- P4.7 Dynamic watchlists & portfolio overlays (`mode: manual|screen|agent|theme|portfolio`)

## Exit criteria

- A thread titled "Iran-US Trade Tensions" works the same as a ticker thread but anchors on a `theme` subject.
- Analyze template runs produce a memo that can be "Added to chat" as a referenced artifact (same snapshot) without re-running the analyst.
- Home feed is cluster-deduped so the same story across three outlets shows as one card.
- Right-rail activity is distinct from findings (findings are durable product; activity is execution telemetry).
- A Reddit thread in `signals` renders via shared evidence-backed blocks, never raw social text panes.
- A screen-derived dynamic watchlist regenerates membership from the saved screen definition, not a frozen row cache.

## Invariants this epic primarily guards

- **I2** / **I3** — specialized social blocks still bind to evidence-backed claims, not raw text.
- **I5** — shared artifact flow reuses sealed snapshots; add-to-chat does not re-seal.

## Why this ordering

Without P4 the app works but doesn't *look* like the videos. Home-feed dedupe only makes sense after P3's `ClaimCluster` exists. Analyze templates only make sense after P2.3 block registry and P2.4 snapshot contract exist. Shared artifact flow only works once snapshots are the sealed handoff unit.

title: P4.1 Themes & macro subjects
description:
# P4.1 — Themes & macro subjects

## What

First-class research subjects beyond issuer/instrument/listing: `Theme`, `ThemeMembership`, `macro_topic`. Themes support manual, rule-based, or inferred membership. Theme chats ("Iran-US Trade Tensions", "AI Chips Alpha") sit in the same right-rail thread list as ticker chats.

## Why

The videos show theme/macro chats as equal peers to ticker chats. Without themes as first-class subjects, users either jam theme research into ticker chats (losing subject discipline) or the app grows a separate "theme" UI track that reinvents subject hydration, snapshotting, and agent universes.

## Contract / acceptance

- `Theme { theme_id, name, description, membership_mode: manual|rule_based|inferred, membership_spec?, active_from, active_to? }`
- `ThemeMembership { theme_id, subject_ref, score, rationale_claim_ids[], effective_at, expires_at? }`
- Themes and macro topics are valid `SubjectRef.kind` values (`theme`, `macro_topic`).
- Theme-derived universes may come from screen handoff (P1.4b) — screen-to-theme handoff carries explicit `screen` source reference so membership provenance is explainable.
- Inferred membership uses `ClaimCluster` + `EntityImpact` signals.
- Chat threads with `primary_subject_kind = theme` work end-to-end: pre-resolve, analyst bundle `theme_research`, snapshot, block render — no special-cased code paths.

## Depends on

- P0.3 (resolver extends to themes)
- P1.4b (screen-to-theme handoff)

## Downstream consumers

- P4.4 (Home groups findings by theme tag)
- P4.7 (dynamic watchlists — theme mode)
- P5.1 (agents can scope universe to a theme)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Inferred membership must rebuild safely — explain via `rationale_claim_ids[]` rather than opaque scores.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/themes/ThemeService.ts`
- `services/themes/MembershipEngine.ts`

title: P4.2 Analyze template system
description:
# P4.2 — Analyze template system

## What

Saved, template-driven analysis workflow with editable instructions, selectable source categories, optional added peer/benchmark subjects, a structured memo layout, reruns, and "Add results to chat". Renders via the same `BlockRegistry` as chat.

## Why

Analyze templates encode recurring research patterns (e.g., "Quarterly earnings memo", "Competitive snapshot", "Thesis review"). The videos show Analyze as a distinct top-level workspace that produces memo-style artifacts — not "chat with a symbol loaded."

## Contract / acceptance

- `AnalyzeTemplate { template_id, user_id, name, prompt_template, source_categories[], added_subject_refs[], block_layout_hint?, peer_policy?, disclosure_policy?, version }`.
- Users select **source categories** (company profile, financials annual/quarterly, news, reddit, etc.) and **layout** — NOT raw internal tool names. Orchestrator maps categories to safe bundles + tool policies.
- Analyze runs via the `analyze_template_run` bundle (PX.2 tool runtime).
- Structured memo renders through `BlockRegistry`: `section` containers at the top level, nested chart/table/rich_text/sources blocks.
- Reruns produce a new sealed snapshot — old memo stays readable at its original snapshot.
- "Add results to chat" hands off the sealed snapshot into a chat thread with shared provenance (reuse, not re-run).

## Depends on

- P1.3b (symbol → Analyze handoff)
- P2.3 (block registry)
- P2.4 (snapshot assembler — memos are sealed artifacts)

## Downstream consumers

- P4.3 (shared artifact flow — Analyze memos added to chat)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Do NOT expose raw internal tool names to users. Keep UI vocabulary at "source categories" + "layout hints".
- Peer policy controls whether added subjects are benchmarks, peers, or out-of-universe — different semantics for comparison blocks.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/analyze/TemplateRepo.ts`
- `services/analyze/TemplateRunner.ts`
- `app/analyze/*`

title: P4.3 Shared artifact flow (add-to-chat)
description:
# P4.3 — Shared artifact flow (add-to-chat)

## What

The cross-surface handoff that lets an Analyze memo (or a finding, or a block) be added to a chat thread as a referenced artifact — sharing the sealed snapshot rather than re-running the analyst. Keeps provenance coherent across Home, Analyze, and Chat.

## Why

The videos show "Add results to chat" as a first-class gesture. If adding were a re-run, users would get inconsistent numbers between the memo and the added chat message. If it were a screenshot, interactivity would break. Sharing the sealed snapshot preserves both interactivity and I2 (narrative matches visuals across surfaces).

## Contract / acceptance

- An Analyze memo, a Finding, or a selected block can be "added to chat".
- Added artifacts carry their original `snapshot_id` — chat messages with added artifacts reference the same sealed manifest.
- In-snapshot transforms on added artifacts work inside the chat message (e.g., range change on a `perf_comparison` block).
- Out-of-snapshot interactions produce an explicit refresh prompt (not a silent refetch).
- `chat_messages.blocks` may include artifact-reference blocks pointing to the shared snapshot.
- The chat thread's `latest_snapshot_id` advances only when the chat itself produces a new snapshot — adding an artifact does NOT overwrite thread snapshot.

## Depends on

- P2.4 (sealed snapshots are the transfer unit)
- P1.3b (Analyze entry handoff pattern)

## Downstream consumers

- P6.4 (export/share policy decides what private overlay state travels with shared artifacts)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Entitlement channels: a fact may be `app`-only but not `export` — shared artifacts must respect this at handoff.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/artifact/SharedArtifact.ts`
- `app/chat/ArtifactReferenceBlock.tsx`

title: P4.4 Home feed ranking & cluster dedupe
description:
# P4.4 — Home feed ranking & cluster dedupe

## What

The cross-agent findings surface: aggregates `Finding` objects across active agents, dedupes by `ClaimCluster`, groups by theme/subject where appropriate, ranks by recency + severity + user relevance, collapses to headline + source + subject/theme tag + timestamp. Plus market pulse, watchlist movers, agent summaries, pinned screens.

## Why

The videos' Home tab is findings-first, not a raw news page. The three hard parts are: cluster dedupe (one story in three outlets = one card), ranking (recency + severity + relevance), and not becoming a raw newsfeed.

## Contract / acceptance

- Query findings across all active agents for the user.
- Dedupe on `ClaimCluster` — one card per cluster, support_count shown.
- Group by theme/subject where appropriate.
- Rank: `score = w_recency * recency + w_severity * severity + w_relevance * user_affinity` (weights configurable).
- Collapsed card: headline + primary source + subject/theme tag + timestamp + severity badge.
- Expandable detail surfaces `summary_blocks` (Block[]) from the finding.
- Secondary sections: market pulse, watchlist movers, agent summaries, pinned screens.
- Findings deep-link into the right deterministic surface — symbol detail, theme thread, or Analyze — rather than inventing custom readouts per card.
- Home renders via the same `BlockRegistry` — `finding_card` block, not custom HTML.

## Depends on

- P1.3 (stable symbol-tab destinations for deep-links)
- P3.4 (claim clusters)
- P5.3 (findings exist)

## Downstream consumers

- PX.3 (frontend rendering)

## Considerations / gotchas

- User affinity signals come from watchlist membership, portfolio holdings, and thread history — respect privacy.
- Do NOT let a high-volume low-severity cluster (e.g., routine analyst upgrades) crowd out a critical finding.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/home/FeedService.ts`
- `services/home/Ranker.ts`
- `/v1/home/*` handlers
- `app/home/Home.tsx`

title: P4.5 Right-rail activity stream
description:
# P4.5 — Right-rail activity stream

## What

The shell-owned right-rail surface for live agent execution telemetry: `Reading`, `Investigating`, `Found`, `Dismissed` stages. Distinct from findings — activity is execution telemetry, findings are durable product artifacts.

## Why

The videos show the right-rail filling with live activity as agents work: "Reading 10-Q for AAPL", "Investigating claim cluster X", "Found: guidance update". Keeping this distinct from findings prevents the user from conflating transient telemetry with durable research output.

## Contract / acceptance

- `RunActivity { run_activity_id, agent_id, stage: reading|investigating|found|dismissed, subject_refs[], source_refs[], summary, ts }`.
- Right-rail renders a reverse-chronological stream of recent activities.
- Activities emit in near-real-time via SSE while an agent run is active; persist to table for later scrollback.
- Activities are NOT findings. `Finding` is durable product; `RunActivity` is execution state.
- Dismissed activities indicate agent evaluated and rejected — they still emit to the stream for transparency.
- The right-rail slot is shell-owned (P0.1); surfaces opt in.

## Depends on

- P0.1 (shell-owned right-rail slot)
- P5.2 (queue runner emits RunActivity during runs)

## Downstream consumers

- (leaf)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Don't flood the stream — rate-limit per-agent to prevent one noisy agent from drowning others.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `app/shell/RightRail.tsx`
- `app/agents/ActivityStream.tsx`
- `services/agents/ActivityEmitter.ts`

title: P4.6 Specialized social & news blocks
description:
# P4.6 — Specialized social & news blocks

## What

Interactive, evidence-bound behavior for block kinds that surface social and news content: `news_cluster`, `sentiment_trend`, `mention_volume`. These kinds are defined in P2.3's initial block catalog; P4.6 implements their **domain-specific behavior** when rendered inside the `signals` symbol tab or elsewhere in social/news contexts.

**Scope distinction vs P2.3:**
- P2.3 defines the block **schema** and implements **basic rendering shells** for all 20 block kinds.
- P4.6 implements the **specialized interactive behavior**: click-through to ClaimCluster detail, sentiment aggregation over time, mention-volume series with cache-safe transforms, and the composition patterns that let `signals` render evidence-backed content without raw social panes.

## Why

The videos show Reddit + news pulse as first-class symbol content, but rendering them as raw text panes would break I4 (analyst never sees raw text; renderer shows evidence-backed structure). P4.6 makes sure these blocks always compose clusters, trend series, and cited evidence — never free-form social-feed scrolls.

## Contract / acceptance

- `news_cluster { cluster_id, headline, claim_refs[], document_refs[] }` — bound to a `ClaimCluster` with supporting documents; click expands to evidence bundle.
- `sentiment_trend { series[] }` — aggregated sentiment over time for a subject or cluster.
- `mention_volume { series[] }` — raw mention counts (prose must include disclosure: mentions ≠ impact).
- All three reuse the same series semantics + cache contract as P1.1b trend-style blocks.
- `signals` tab composes these evidence-backed blocks — NOT raw social-text panes or provider-specific mini-pages.

## Depends on

- P2.3 (block schema + basic renderer shells)
- P1.1b (trend series semantics + cache identity rules)
- P1.2b (key stats + consensus aggregate envelopes for narrative)
- P3.4 (claim clusters)
- P3.6 (evidence bundles for click-through)

## Downstream consumers

- (leaf — these are rendered blocks)

## Considerations / gotchas

- `mention_volume` is easy to misinterpret as "popularity" — include disclosure about the distinction from impact.
- Sentiment is inherently noisy from social sources — expose confidence + source composition.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `app/blocks/NewsCluster.tsx` (specialized behavior layer)
- `app/blocks/SentimentTrend.tsx`
- `app/blocks/MentionVolume.tsx`
- `app/symbol/signals/SignalsTab.tsx` (composition)

title: P4.7 Dynamic watchlists & portfolio overlays
description:
# P4.7 — Dynamic watchlists & portfolio overlays

## What

Watchlist derivation modes beyond manual: `screen`, `agent`, `theme`, `portfolio`. Plus portfolio overlays across watchlists, themes, screens, and subject views — driven by P1.5's overlay input contract.

## Why

The videos show watchlists that auto-populate from screens, themes, and portfolios. The derivation modes must reuse replayable definitions (not frozen row caches) to stay accurate. Portfolio overlays need to layer held-state on any subject-collection view without rewriting base surfaces.

## Contract / acceptance

- `Watchlist.mode ∈ { manual, screen, agent, theme, portfolio }`.
- `screen` mode regenerates membership from the saved screen definition — no frozen rows.
- `agent` mode mirrors the agent's universe.
- `theme` mode mirrors `ThemeMembership` for a theme.
- `portfolio` mode mirrors a portfolio's holdings.
- Portfolio overlays use the overlay input contract (P1.5) — read models keyed by subject × contributing portfolio.
- Overlays merge portfolio context with watchlists, themes, screens, and subject views consistently.
- Multiple portfolios with different base currencies stay distinct (no silent FX netting).
- Users can see both watchlist and held state on the same row without collapsing concepts.

## Depends on

- P0.4b (manual baseline)
- P1.5b (shared surface behaviors)
- P1.4b (saved screens as replayable sources)

## Downstream consumers

- P5.1 (agents can target dynamic watchlists as universe)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Derivation modes recompute membership — make cost obvious to users (e.g., a screen-derived list with 500 members is expensive to refresh).

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/watchlists/DynamicMembership.ts`
- `services/overlays/OverlayComposer.ts`
- `app/watchlists/DynamicWatchlist.tsx`

title: Epic P5 — Background agents
description:
# Epic P5 — Background agents

## Goal

Put thesis agents behind the Home feed: agent CRUD with thesis + universe + cadence + alert rules + watermarks; a queue runner with idempotent runs; finding generation with severity scoring; alert evaluation with approval gating; and notification channels (push/email/SMS/digest). Agents run reader extraction over deltas, update clusters, run analyst on relevant evidence, and emit `Finding` + `RunActivity`.

## Child tasks

- P5.1 Agent CRUD, scheduling, watermark storage (transactional advance)
- P5.2 Queue runner & idempotent agent runs (Cloudflare Queues are at-least-once)
- P5.3 Finding generation & severity scoring (sealed snapshot per finding)
- P5.4 Alert evaluation & approval gating (`create_alert` pending-action flow)
- P5.5 Notification channels — web push, email, SMS, mobile push, digest

## Exit criteria

- An agent with cadence `daily` and a thesis about AI chips produces at least one finding after a filing drop.
- Re-running the same queue message does not duplicate findings or double-advance watermarks.
- A `create_alert` tool call from chat returns a pending-action id; nothing fires until the user approves.
- Findings dedupe via `ClaimCluster` on the Home feed.
- `RunActivity` rows emit with stages `reading | investigating | found | dismissed` and drive the right-rail.

## Invariants this epic primarily guards

- **I7** (side effects require approval) — alerts and agent creation.
- **I4** (analyst never sees raw text) — agent loop still uses reader for extraction.

## Why this ordering

Agents are the most operationally complex subsystem. They require P3 (evidence plane), P2.4 (snapshot assembler), and PX.2 (approval flow) to already exist. Putting agents last keeps the early graph debuggable.

## Gotchas

- Cloudflare Queues provide at-least-once delivery and do NOT guarantee publish order. Runs must be idempotent. Watermark advance must not assume FIFO.
- Low-confidence extractions must land in a reviewer queue (P6.3), not silently auto-promote.

title: P5.1 Agent CRUD, scheduling, watermark storage
description:
# P5.1 — Agent CRUD, scheduling, watermark storage

## What

User-owned background research agents: CRUD, thesis/universe/cadence/alert-rules/prompt-template config, transactional watermark storage that tracks "what have we already processed" for each agent, and the approval flow for `create_agent` (approval-sensitive tool).

## Why

Agents are the engine behind Home. Without watermark discipline, re-runs either duplicate work or miss deltas. Without approval gating on `create_agent`, the analyst could spawn agents silently — an invariant I7 violation.

## Contract / acceptance

- `Agent { agent_id, user_id, name, thesis, universe (SubjectRef[] | dynamic: screen|theme|portfolio), source_policy, cadence, prompt_template, alert_rules[], watermarks, enabled }`.
- Watermarks advance **transactionally** after each successful run — never before.
- `create_agent` is approval-sensitive (per tool registry) — returns `pending_action_id` until user confirms.
- Universe may reference a dynamic watchlist (P4.7) — regenerated per run.
- Alert rules compile to evaluable predicates over Facts, Claims, Events, Impacts.
- `cadence` is human-readable (`hourly | daily | on-filing | ...`) and compiled to a schedule.

## Depends on

- P0.4b (baseline user-owned list model)
- PX.2 (approval-sensitive tool flow)

## Downstream consumers

- P5.2 (queue runner reads due agents)
- P5.3 (findings tied to agent runs)
- P5.4 (alert rules evaluated per run)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Watermarks are JSON by shape — make them versioned so schema evolution doesn't re-process history.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/agents/AgentRepo.ts`
- `services/agents/AlertRuleCompiler.ts`
- `/v1/agents/*` handlers

title: P5.2 Queue runner & idempotent agent runs
description:
# P5.2 — Queue runner & idempotent agent runs

## What

Background runner that consumes scheduled agent due-events from a queue (Cloudflare Queues or equivalent), runs the agent loop (ingest deltas → cluster → score relevance → analyst run → emit findings + activity → evaluate alerts → advance watermark), and does all of this **idempotently**.

## Why

Cloudflare Queues provide at-least-once delivery and do NOT guarantee publish order. Re-processing a message must not duplicate findings or double-advance watermarks. This is a hard constraint.

## Contract / acceptance

- Queue handler accepts an agent-run message with agent_id + run_id.
- Duplicate run_id is detected and short-circuits with no side effects.
- Agent loop: load due agent → read deltas since watermark → reader extraction → cluster/corroborate/update impacts → score relevance to thesis → analyst on structured evidence → emit findings → emit activities → evaluate alerts → advance watermark transactionally.
- Watermark advance and finding emission share one transaction.
- Per-agent concurrency cap (default 1) prevents parallel runs for the same agent.

## Depends on

- P5.1 (agent model + watermarks)
- P3.2 (ingest pipeline)
- P3.4 (clustering)
- P2.4 (snapshot assembler — findings are sealed)

## Downstream consumers

- P5.3 (findings)
- P5.4 (alerts)
- P5.5 (notifications)
- P4.5 (activity stream)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Do not assume FIFO ordering on queue messages. Out-of-order deliveries must converge to the right state.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/agents/QueueRunner.ts`
- `services/agents/AgentLoop.ts`

title: P5.3 Finding generation & severity scoring
description:
# P5.3 — Finding generation & severity scoring

## What

The service that produces `Finding` objects during agent runs: sealed snapshot per finding, subject refs, claim_cluster_ids, severity (`low | medium | high | critical`), headline, and `summary_blocks: Block[]` rendering via the same `BlockRegistry`.

## Why

Findings are the durable product artifact of agents. They live on Home, can be added to chat (P4.3), can trigger alerts (P5.4), and must reference sealed snapshots so their content is reproducible.

## Contract / acceptance

- `Finding { finding_id, agent_id, snapshot_id, subject_refs[], claim_cluster_ids[], severity, headline, summary_blocks[], created_at }`.
- Every finding references a sealed snapshot (finding generation calls P2.4).
- Severity scoring combines evidence strength (trust tier, corroboration count), impact (direction × channel × horizon), and thesis relevance.
- `summary_blocks` validates against the block schema — just like chat/Analyze.
- Findings dedupe on Home via `ClaimCluster`.

## Depends on

- P2.4 (snapshot assembler)
- P3.4 (claim clusters)

## Downstream consumers

- P4.4 (Home feed)
- P5.4 (alert evaluation)
- P5.5 (notification composition)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Severity thresholds should be tuned against the golden-eval suite (PX.1), not set arbitrarily.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/agents/FindingGenerator.ts`
- `services/agents/SeverityScorer.ts`

title: P5.4 Alert evaluation & approval gating
description:
# P5.4 — Alert evaluation & approval gating

## What

Compiles per-agent `alert_rules[]` to evaluable predicates, evaluates them against findings + facts + claims + events per run, and enforces approval gating for `create_alert` (approval-sensitive tool).

## Why

Alerts are side-effecting (they send notifications). Without approval gating, the analyst could create alerts silently. The rule-compile layer also ensures alert rules are declarative rather than opaque code paths — reviewable by the user.

## Contract / acceptance

- Alert rules are declarative structures (subject scope, condition predicates, channels).
- Rule evaluation is deterministic and explainable — each firing points to the claim/fact/event/finding that triggered it.
- `create_alert` returns `pending_action_id` until user confirms.
- Rule firing writes an `alerts_fired` log row; notification composition is P5.5.

## Depends on

- P5.1 (agent alert_rules config)
- P5.2 (evaluation runs inside the agent loop)
- P5.3 (findings are inputs)
- PX.2 (approval-sensitive tool flow)

## Downstream consumers

- P5.5 (notifications consume fired alerts)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Allow dry-run: a user creating a rule can test it against historical data before enabling.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/agents/AlertEvaluator.ts`
- `services/agents/AlertRuleCompiler.ts`

title: P5.5 Notification channels (push/email/SMS/digest)
description:
# P5.5 — Notification channels (push/email/SMS/digest)

## What

Notification delivery across web push, email, SMS, mobile push, and scheduled digests. Channels honor per-fact `entitlement_channels[]` — some facts are `app`-only and may not egress via email or push.

## Why

Notifications move content outside the app boundary. `entitlement_channels` is how license/compliance controls propagate to egress. Without this gate, a delayed-pricing fact could be emailed in a way that violates the data provider's license.

## Contract / acceptance

- Channels: web push, email, SMS, mobile push, digest.
- Every outbound notification checks `entitlement_channels[]` on referenced facts; `app`-only facts cannot be rendered in notifications that egress.
- Digests batch multiple findings per channel; respect user preference on cadence.
- User can configure channels per-agent and globally.

## Depends on

- P5.4 (fired alerts are inputs)

## Downstream consumers

- (leaf — egress boundary)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Throttle aggressively. A burst of agents firing simultaneously must not produce hundreds of notifications.
- SMS is expensive — default off, opt-in only.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/notifications/Channels/*`
- `services/notifications/DigestScheduler.ts`

title: Implement structured tool_call_log writer
description: What: Every tool call writes tool_call_logs. Why: Observability baseline. Contract: Row carries tool_name, args hash, result_hash, duration, status. Verification: Run tool; row appears.

title: Implement data_ref resolver (canonical name standardization)
description: What: Standardize on data_ref (NOT dataRef or queryRef) — historical language is aliased but not persisted. Why: Schema hygiene. Contract: All blocks use data_ref. Verification: Lint across block emitters.

title: Implement parent_document_id for threaded sources
description: What: Support Reddit threads + transcript segments via parent_document_id. Why: Conversation context matters for claim extraction. Contract: A reply references its parent document. Verification: Ingest a Reddit thread; parent-child edges resolve.

title: Implement right-rail activity renderer with rate limiting
description: What: Reverse-chronological renderer with per-agent rate limits. Why: One noisy agent must not drown others. Contract: Per-agent rate cap configurable. Verification: Simulate burst from one agent; others still visible.

title: Implement fast-path detection (quote-only short-circuit)
description: What: Quote-only requests bypass the analyst loop and produce a deterministic response. Why: Token + latency savings. Contract: 'AAPL price' runs in <100ms without analyst call. Verification: Instrument the path.

title: Implement theme chats as primary_subject_kind='theme'
description: What: Chat threads anchored on themes work end-to-end (pre-resolve, theme_research bundle, snapshot, render). Why: No special-case theme code paths. Contract: Ticker chat and theme chat share the same pipeline. Verification: Create theme chat; get block response.

title: Implement queue aging monitor
description: What: Stale queue items surface to operators. Why: Queue starvation masks quality issues. Contract: Alert on items older than threshold. Verification: Simulate stale item.

title: Implement cross-agent finding query + cluster dedupe
description: What: Aggregate findings across active agents; collapse by ClaimCluster. Why: Dedupe is the whole point of Home. Contract: One story in three outlets = one card with support_count=3. Verification: Seed fixtures with 3 findings sharing cluster.

title: Implement BlockRegistry keyed by kind with 20 block renderers
description: What: React components for all 20 block kinds registered to a single registry. Why: Shared registry powers chat, Analyze, findings. Contract: Same component instance serves all three surfaces. Verification: Render sample of each block in isolation tests.
close_reason: Superseded by 4 granular renderer-group subtasks for better claimability

title: Implement AnalyzeTemplate CRUD
description: What: Template repo + API. Why: Templates are user-owned artifacts. Contract: Template includes prompt_template, source_categories, added_subject_refs, layout_hint. Verification: Create/read/update/delete.

title: Implement EntityLinker with canonical-identity consultation
description: What: Entity linker consulting resolver for canonical ids. Why: Mentions must point at canonical subjects. Contract: Ambiguous mentions preserve ranked candidates. Verification: Article mentioning 'Apple' resolves to issuer + relevant listings.

title: Implement bundle_id immutability + get_evidence_bundle
description: What: Bundles are immutable; bundle_id always resolves to same content. Why: Block source_refs reference stable bundles. Contract: Second fetch returns identical payload. Verification: Re-fetch determinism test.

title: Implement SSE-driven incremental block rendering
description: What: block.began -> skeleton; delta -> update; completed -> finalize. Why: Streaming feel. Contract: Partial blocks render progressively. Verification: Throttle SSE; watch skeleton -> content.

title: Implement LEI + non-US ISIN resolution
description: What: Resolver handles LEI and non-US ISINs. Why: International coverage. Contract: LEI lookup resolves to issuer. Verification: Known LEI resolves.

title: Implement press release + transcript + news ingestion
description: What: Pipelines for issuer press, earnings transcripts, news articles. Why: Secondary-source corroboration. Contract: Per-kind canonicalization + publisher attribution. Verification: Ingest sample of each kind.

title: Implement portfolio overlay composition across views
description: What: Overlay composer merges portfolio context with watchlists/themes/screens/subject views. Why: Unified held-state layering. Contract: Overlay inputs keyed by subject × contributing portfolio. Verification: Subject in multiple portfolios shows separate contributions.

title: Implement verifier (refs, sources, units, periods, disclosures, approvals)
description: What: Exhaustive check list before seal. Why: The invariants become real here. Contract: Failure modes each log verifier_fail_logs with reason_code. Verification: Golden set covers each failure mode.

title: Implement ClaimCluster + canonical_signature
description: What: Claim clustering with canonical_signature for dedupe. Why: Home-feed dedupe across sources. Contract: Similar claims across outlets join one cluster. Verification: Same story in three outlets = one cluster with support_count=3.

title: Implement cost-class budget gate
description: What: Per-turn caps on low/medium/high-cost tool calls. Why: Cost containment. Contract: Exceeded budget produces partial-answer path + note. Verification: Stress test.

title: Epic P6 — Hard cases & scale
description:
# Epic P6 — Hard cases & scale

## Goal

Harden the coverage and operational footprint: segment extraction refinement (issuer-specific extension facts and non-GAAP logic), non-US identity/data/coverage expansion, a reviewer queue for low-confidence extractions, the export/share policy (what private overlay state travels with a shared artifact), and scale hardening (cache layout, freshness audits, provider bottleneck investigation).

## Child tasks

- P6.1 Segment extraction refinement (XBRL extensions, non-GAAP, axis evolution)
- P6.2 Non-US identity/data/coverage gaps (beyond SEC EDGAR)
- P6.3 Reviewer queues for low-confidence extractions and disputed promotions
- P6.4 Export & share policy (overlay state, entitlement channels)
- P6.5 Scale hardening (cache identity audits, provider bottlenecks, snapshot storage)

## Exit criteria

- Segment facts survive segment redefinitions with explicit `definition_as_of` pinning.
- Non-US issuers resolve to canonical identity (LEI, ISIN) and at least one non-US exchange is first-class.
- Extraction failures with confidence below threshold enter reviewer queue rather than auto-promoting.
- Shared artifacts (chat messages, Analyze memos) respect `entitlement_channels` per fact.
- Cache hit rate is monitored; cache identity (subject × range × interval × basis × normalization × freshness boundary) is auditable.

## Why this is last

P6 is ambient hardening. Its work applies to data and systems already operational. Scheduling it earlier either blocks on pieces that don't exist yet or produces hardening that must be redone once shapes change.

## Invariants this epic primarily guards

- **I1** / **I3** — promotion rules stay honest at scale; reviewer queue catches ambiguous cases.
- **I7** — export/share respects entitlement channels (some facts are not exportable).

title: P6.1 Segment extraction refinement
description:
# P6.1 — Segment extraction refinement

## What

Beyond the SEC companyfacts aggregates: extraction of segment data, issuer-specific XBRL extension facts, non-GAAP reconciliations, and segment-axis evolution across filings. Preserves axis, definitions, and coverage warnings.

## Why

The SEC `data.sec.gov` companyfacts API explicitly excludes segment data, issuer-specific extension facts, and non-GAAP logic. A naive companyfacts-only implementation will ship financials tables that look complete but are missing the segment breakdowns users expect — the exact surface shown in the videos.

## Contract / acceptance

- Segment extraction reads XBRL with extension support.
- Segment-axis evolution across filings preserves `definition_as_of` pinning so segment redefinitions are visible.
- Non-GAAP reconciliations extract with explicit GAAP-to-non-GAAP mapping.
- Coverage warnings surface on segment breakdowns — e.g., "segment definition changed Q2 2024".
- Extensions extractor operates in reader audience (raw XBRL text may contain narrative).

## Depends on

- P1.2b (segment aggregation API)
- P3.2 (extraction pipeline)

## Downstream consumers

- P1.3 (financials tab segment renderers)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Some issuers redefine segments multiple times — historical comparability requires transparent handling.

title: P6.2 Non-US identity, data, and coverage gaps
description:
# P6.2 — Non-US identity, data, and coverage gaps

## What

Expand beyond US SEC EDGAR: non-US issuer identity (LEI, non-US ISINs, local identifiers), non-US exchange listings, local accounting standards (IFRS mapping in `metrics.canonical_source_class`), and provider/vendor coverage for international markets.

## Why

The videos focus on US markets, but the identity layer already supports `issuer.domicile` and `listing.mic` for a reason. International coverage is ambient rather than a Phase 0 requirement — hence P6.

## Contract / acceptance

- Non-US issuer resolution works for at least one major international exchange (e.g., LSE, TSE).
- IFRS metric mappings preserve `canonical_source_class: 'ifrs'` distinct from GAAP.
- Listings in non-USD trading currencies render without silent FX conversion (I7 for implicit conversions).
- Coverage warnings surface when non-US data is thinner than US data for a comparison.

## Depends on

- P1.1b (basis/coverage rules)
- P1.2 (statement normalization)
- P1.2b (aggregation outputs)

## Downstream consumers

- (leaf)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Do NOT silently FX-convert when comparing US and non-US listings — make the currency visible or force explicit normalization.

title: P6.3 Reviewer queues for low-confidence extractions
description:
# P6.3 — Reviewer queues for low-confidence extractions

## What

Human-in-the-loop queue for extractions below the confidence threshold: low-confidence candidate facts, disputed claims, ambiguous entity links. Reviewers can approve, reject, or edit before promotion.

## Why

Silent auto-promotion of low-confidence extractions violates I3. A reviewer queue is the escape hatch that preserves promotion discipline without either dropping signal or blocking the whole pipeline.

## Contract / acceptance

- Queue rows reference the source document + extracted candidate.
- Reviewers can approve (promote per rules), reject (mark as rejected), or edit (revise fields + re-promote).
- Queue aging is monitored — stale queue items surface to operators.
- Queue throughput is capped per-reviewer to prevent rubber-stamping.

## Depends on

- P3.5 (promotion rules + threshold config)

## Downstream consumers

- (operations leaf)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Reviewer bias: two reviewers reaching different conclusions is a signal the threshold is wrong, not that one reviewer is right.

title: P6.4 Export & share policy
description:
# P6.4 — Export & share policy

## What

Policy framework controlling what travels when a user exports or shares an artifact (chat message, Analyze memo, finding): which facts may egress via which channels (`entitlement_channels[]`), which private overlay state (watchlist/held badges) travels with shared artifacts, and how disclosures are preserved.

## Why

Shared artifacts can leave the app boundary. Without explicit policy, entitlements get violated (delayed pricing emailed out) and private overlay state leaks (user A shares a memo showing user A's portfolio held-state).

## Contract / acceptance

- Every fact carries `entitlement_channels[]`; export/share respects it.
- Private overlay state (watchlist/held badges, portfolio quantities) is stripped by default from shared artifacts.
- Disclosures are preserved on exported artifacts (delayed pricing, candidate-vs-authoritative, etc.).
- Share URL artifacts resolve via sealed `snapshot_id` — the recipient sees the same evidence the author saw.

## Depends on

- P1.5b (user-owned state distinct from canonical content)
- P4.3 (shared artifact flow)

## Downstream consumers

- (leaf)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Print/PDF export must honor the same policy — not just web share.

title: P6.5 Scale hardening
description:
# P6.5 — Scale hardening

## What

Ambient performance work at scale: cache identity audits (P1.1b cache layout), provider bottleneck investigation, snapshot storage cost, tool-call-log retention, query plan audits on `facts` / `claims` / `snapshots`, and read-replica topology.

## Why

P6.5 applies to systems already operational. Doing it earlier produces hardening against a moving target.

## Contract / acceptance

- Cache hit rate is monitored; cache identity (P1.1b) is auditable via logs.
- Provider bottleneck audits identify slow/rate-limited vendors and produce fallback plans.
- Snapshot storage growth rate is tracked; cold snapshots move to archival storage.
- Tool call log retention policy documented; older rows archive or summarize.
- Query plan audits on partitioned tables confirm index usage.

## Depends on

- P1.1 (provider-neutral boundary)
- P1.1b (cache identity rules)

## Downstream consumers

- (operations leaf)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Don't optimize before measuring. Baselines first.

title: Implement web push + email channels with entitlement gating
description: What: Deliver via web push + email; skip facts with entitlement_channels excluding the channel. Why: License compliance. Contract: app-only facts never leak to email. Verification: Attempt to egress app-only fact; blocked.

title: Implement audience enforcement (reader vs analyst)
description: What: Static + runtime gates preventing analyst from receiving raw document bytes. Why: I4 is too important to be untested. Contract: Attempted analyst call with raw_blob_url rejected. Verification: Adversarial tests.

title: Implement SSE reconnect with last-seq resume
description: What: Client can reconnect with Last-Event-ID and receive missed events. Why: Flaky networks must not lose assistant output. Contract: Reconnect yields events strictly after the resumed sequence. Verification: Simulate 5s disconnect mid-stream.

title: Epic P1 — Terminal core
description:
# Epic P1 — Terminal core

## Goal

Build the deterministic research terminal layer: provider-neutral market data, fundamentals (statement normalization + stats/segment/consensus aggregation), symbol detail surfaces (overview, financials, earnings, holders, signals), the screener query model + surface, and portfolio/watchlist basics. At the end of P1, the app is usable as a read-only finance terminal even without chat.

## Child tasks

- P1.1 Market data service (quote/bar provider abstraction)
- P1.1b Adjusted series query & caching semantics
- P1.2 Fundamentals service (statement & metric normalization)
- P1.2b Stats, segment, and consensus aggregations
- P1.3 Symbol detail — overview / financials / earnings core tabs
- P1.3b Symbol detail — holders, signals, Analyze entry integration
- P1.4 Screener query model & result contract
- P1.4b Screener surface flow & saved-screen handoff
- P1.5 Portfolio holdings model & overlay inputs
- P1.5b Portfolio & watchlist first surface behaviors

## Exit criteria

- Quote and bar retrieval are provider-agnostic: swapping providers requires no downstream changes.
- Series responses carry explicit `basis`, `normalization`, `as_of`, `coverage_start`, `source_id`.
- Statement tables never silently mix `as_reported` and `as_restated`.
- Segment facts expose `axis`, `definitions`, and `coverage_warnings` (never flattened into issuer-level tables).
- Screener runs publicly; saving a screen is gated to session.
- Watchlist membership is idempotent at canonical subject level (not ticker).
- Symbol detail navigation preserves subject context and shell chrome across tab transitions.

## Invariants this epic primarily guards

- **I1** (no displayed number without a backing Fact/Computation row) — enforced by fundamentals + market data services funneling through Fact/Computation.
- **I2** (narrative and visuals must agree) — metric/segment aggregations are the value layer that chat + blocks will reference by id.
- Identity discipline — downstream modules only consume resolved subject refs, never raw tickers.

## Why this ordering

P2 (chat) depends on `get_series`, `get_statement_facts`, `get_key_stats`, `get_segment_facts`, `get_analyst_consensus`, `get_eps_surprise`, `get_peer_set` already returning canonical facts with provenance. Without P1 complete, the analyst tool loop would either have to invent a shadow data layer or return ungrounded numbers — both invariant violations. P4 (Home, Analyze) and P5 (agents) layer narrative atop the same terminal-core services.

title: P1.1 Market data service (quote & bar provider abstraction)
description:
# P1.1 — Market data service (quote & bar provider abstraction)

## What

The sole internal boundary for quote and bar provider interaction. Downstream consumers (symbol detail, chat tools, watchlist rows, screener) call the market data service or analyst tools — never provider SDKs, raw provider endpoints, or provider-specific payload helpers. Provider adapters normalize vendor-specific shapes, identifiers, rate limits, and availability quirks into stable internal market records before they reach downstream consumers.

## Why

Provider lock-in is the single most common source of reliability risk in finance data. A provider-neutral market-data seam lets us swap providers, run fallbacks, and layer caching without changing consumer code. It also prevents ticker-string shortcuts — consumers must pass listing-appropriate hydrated subject context, not raw ticker text.

## Contract / acceptance

- Quote and bar retrieval start from listing-appropriate hydrated subject context (`listing` SubjectRef), **not** raw tickers, venue text, or provider identifiers.
- Provider-neutral contract preserves `as_of`, `delay_class`, `currency`, `source_id` for all quote/bar responses; bars also carry `adjustment_basis`.
- Quote retrieval: latest price, absolute move, percentage move, freshness/session state — lightweight and snapshot-oriented. Does NOT answer historical range questions, adjusted-series questions, or comparison normalization (those belong to P1.1b).
- Bar retrieval: ordered intraday/historical OHLCV for a listing across a requested range/interval; exposes `adjustment_basis` whenever a response has crossed an adjustment boundary.
- Provider failures, stale data, or missing coverage surface as normalized availability outcomes — never raw provider error payloads leaking to callers.
- When the hydrated subject bundle also carries issuer context, quote retrieval still resolves the active market snapshot from listing context.

## Depends on

- P0.3b (hydrated subject handoff — listing-appropriate)
- P0.5 (schema + `sources` table seeded)

## Downstream consumers

- P1.1b (adjusted series + caching — layered above this)
- P1.3 (symbol detail quote + bar surfaces)
- P2.2 (pre-resolve router uses cost class to choose quote vs bar path)
- P6.5 (scale hardening audits at this boundary)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Corporate actions belong to the market data service but their semantics are deferred to P1.1b. This bead only needs adapters to *not lose* corporate-action info in normalization.
- Venue-sensitive session interpretation (regular vs pre/post market) lives here — don't push it downstream.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/market/adapters/*` (per-provider)
- `services/market/QuoteService.ts`
- `services/market/BarService.ts`
- `/v1/market/quote` + `/v1/market/bars` handlers

title: Implement provider adapter interface + first adapter
description: What: One adapter interface + one working implementation (e.g., Polygon or Tradier). Why: Provider-neutral seam. Contract: Adapter returns normalized shapes; no vendor types leak. Verification: Swap adapter fixture; consumers unchanged.

title: Implement quote contract (as_of, delay_class, currency, source_id)
description: What: Normalized quote response shape. Why: Every displayed quote needs provenance + freshness. Contract: Every quote carries as_of/delay_class/currency/source_id. Verification: Schema assertion in contract tests.

title: Implement bar contract (OHLCV + adjustment_basis)
description: What: Normalized bar response for listing + range + interval. Why: Bars feed charts; adjustment basis must be explicit. Contract: Response exposes requested subject + range + interval + as_of + delay_class + currency + source_id + adjustment_basis. Verification: Schema assertion + test for adjusted vs unadjusted.

title: Implement availability outcome normalization (no raw provider errors)
description: What: Failures and stale data surface as normalized availability outcomes. Why: Raw provider errors leaking to callers couples consumers to vendors. Contract: No caller sees provider-specific error shapes. Verification: Simulate provider 5xx; caller sees normalized envelope.

title: Implement corporate actions handling (splits, dividends, spin-offs)
description: What: Corporate-action events consumed during bar normalization so adjustment_basis is honest. Why: Without CA awareness, adjusted vs unadjusted bars drift silently. Contract: CA events tracked with effective dates; adjustment logic documented per basis. Verification: Split event applied; adjusted bars show continuity across split date.

title: Replace P0.4 quote stub with real market-data wiring
description: What: Swap out the P0.4.3 stub for real P1.1 quote hydration. Why: Stub was a P0 unblocker; forgetting to replace is a latent bug. Contract: Symbol-detail landing uses real quote service; stub removed. Verification: Landing uses live data source_id in response.

title: P1.5b Portfolio & watchlist first surface behaviors
description:
# P1.5b — Portfolio & watchlist first surface behaviors

## What

The first-render surface behaviors for manual watchlists and portfolio-held subjects. Reuses the thin quote-on-read row hydration from P0.4 for both, layering held-state + quantity + cost-basis context on top of the shared quote row skeleton for portfolio-held rows.

## Why

Inventing separate quote-fetch behavior for held subjects creates a second identity model and breaks the shared-subject contract. Saving a subject (watchlist) and holding a subject (portfolio) must remain separate concepts even though their rows share the same hydration skeleton.

## Contract / acceptance

- Manual watchlist + portfolio-held surfaces reuse the same thin quote-on-read row hydration as P0.4.
- Portfolio-held rows layer held-state, quantity, and optional cost-basis context on top of the shared quote row skeleton.
- First surface stays intentionally light: no portfolio analytics dashboard, brokerage position card, or private quote model distinct from symbol entry.
- If a subject is both watchlisted and held, the surface keeps both states visible — does NOT collapse one concept into the other.
- Selecting a row from either a watchlist or portfolio-held surface enters the same symbol-detail route keyed by canonical subject identity.
- Entered subject views may show lightweight saved-state and held-state context plus adjacent actions — context augments existing quote snapshot + subject modules, NOT a portfolio-specific subject shell.
- Watchlist and holdings state remain user-owned overlay context on top of shared subject + market-data contracts.

## Depends on

- P1.5 (holdings model + overlay inputs)
- P0.4b (manual watchlist baseline)

## Downstream consumers

- P4.7 (dynamic watchlists + portfolio overlays — reuse shared row and subject-view augmentation)
- P6.4 (export/share policy — user-owned state stays distinct from canonical content for export gating)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Collapsing watchlist + held states into one flag loses the distinction the videos treat as meaningful.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `app/watchlists/*` (shared row component)
- `app/portfolio/HeldRows.tsx`

title: Implement shared quote-row skeleton across watchlist + held surfaces
description: What: Shared row component used by manual watchlist rows and portfolio-held rows. Why: No second quote identity model. Contract: Same subject renders identical values across both surfaces. Verification: Snapshot test on shared render.

title: Implement both-states visibility (watchlisted AND held)
description: What: Subject that is both watchlisted and held shows both states — does NOT collapse to one. Why: Videos show these as meaningfully distinct. Contract: Subject-detail header shows watchlist + held badges separately. Verification: Add + hold same subject; see both badges.

title: P1.1b Adjusted series query & caching semantics
description:
# P1.1b — Adjusted series query & caching semantics

## What

The market-data surface above raw bar retrieval that produces comparison-ready or interactive time series from canonical listing subject input, with explicit basis/normalization binding and cache identity rules that preserve snapshot correctness.

## Why

Without explicit basis/normalization binding, series responses silently mix adjusted and unadjusted data, and cache hits return stale material when inputs shift. That breaks I1 (provenance), I5 (answer pinned to a moment), and I8 (explicit refresh). This bead is the primary cache surface for the whole app.

## Contract / acceptance

- Series queries bind explicit `subject_refs`, `range`, `interval`, `basis`, `normalization` before execution, caching, and snapshot binding.
- Market-series basis is explicit: `unadjusted` | `split_adjusted` | `split_and_div_adjusted`.
- The service must not silently mix bases inside one returned series set, one chart artifact, or one comparison response.
- Multi-subject performance responses share one basis, one normalization, one `as_of`, one requested range even when coverage start differs by subject.
- Series responses expose effective coverage window and any partial/unavailable history explicitly.
- Cache is internal optimization, not a separate source of truth.
- Cache identity: canonical subject set × range × interval × basis × normalization × freshness boundary.
- Cache hit may reuse prior series only when the request preserves subject membership, basis, normalization, and does not require data newer than request or snapshot `as_of`.
- Cache hits and misses return the same observable contract.
- Changing basis/normalization/peer set/freshness = different request (refresh, not cache mutation).
- In-snapshot transforms may reuse cached series only when the requested range/interval is legal under the sealed snapshot's `allowed_transforms`.
- Changing basis or normalization is out-of-snapshot behavior even if compatible cached material exists.

## Depends on

- P1.1 (quote/bar provider abstraction)

## Downstream consumers

- P1.3 (price charts + comparison modules)
- P2.3 (block registry — interactive blocks need stable series identity)
- P2.4 (snapshot assembler — `series_specs` in manifest)
- P4.6 (specialized social/news blocks — trend-style blocks reuse this)
- P6.2 (non-US coverage — explicit coverage rules)
- P6.5 (scale hardening — cache identity audits)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Cache identity keying is invariant-critical. Getting it wrong = silent snapshot violation.
- Expose the coverage window; never backfill missing periods.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/market/SeriesService.ts`
- `services/market/cache/*`
- `/v1/market/series` handler

title: Implement series query binding (refs, range, interval, basis, normalization)
description: What: Explicit binding of all series-identity dimensions before execution. Why: Implicit defaults silently drift across calls. Contract: Every series request specifies all five dimensions. Verification: Requests missing any dimension are rejected.

title: Implement cache identity (subject × range × interval × basis × normalization × freshness)
description: What: Cache keying strategy preserving snapshot correctness. Why: Wrong keying = silent snapshot violation. Contract: Cache hit possible only when all six dims match AND freshness boundary is respected. Verification: Changing any dim misses cache.

title: Implement in-snapshot transform gate for series reuse
description: What: Legality check for in-snapshot cached-series reuse. Why: Interactivity inside old messages must stay inside the sealed snapshot. Contract: Reuse only when range/interval is listed in allowed_transforms AND range end ≤ as_of. Verification: Test legal vs illegal transforms.

title: Implement explicit coverage window exposure (no backfill)
description: What: Response exposes effective coverage window and partial/unavailable history. Why: Backfilling missing periods invents data. Contract: Responses carry coverage_start and explicit unavailability flags. Verification: A subject with limited history returns coverage_start appropriately.

title: Implement FX normalization for cross-currency comparisons
description: What: When a comparison spans trading currencies, FX conversion is explicit (block-visible + source-backed) or rejected. Why: Spec §19.5: implicit FX conversions are a guardrail violation. Contract: Multi-currency series requires explicit normalization; otherwise response carries both currencies visibly. Verification: Compare AAPL (USD) + ASML (EUR); either FX-normalized block with visible rate, or side-by-side with both currencies.

title: P1.2 Fundamentals service (statement & metric normalization)
description:
# P1.2 — Fundamentals service (statement & metric normalization)

## What

The fundamentals-service layer that turns filing-backed or vendor-backed statement inputs into canonical value objects keyed by metric definitions. Owns the three core statement families (`income`, `balance`, `cashflow`), fiscal-calendar normalization, period/unit/scale normalization, and the mapping from normalized statement lines to canonical `Metric` definitions.

## Why

Displayed statement values must resolve to `Fact` rows when directly observed or promoted, and to `Computation` rows when deterministically derived. Without the fundamentals service as a hard boundary, every surface invents its own statement-cleanup logic, and I1 (no displayed number without a backing row) is silently violated.

## Contract / acceptance

- Statement reads begin from issuer-appropriate subject context, not listing identity or ticker-only lookup.
- Three statement families normalized explicitly: `income`, `balance`, `cashflow`.
- Statement basis is explicit: `as_reported` | `as_restated` — must not be silently merged.
- Period selection, fiscal labels, scale normalization, unit normalization are part of this contract, not caller cleanup.
- `Metric` is a definition object (not a mutable value store).
- Displayed statement values resolve to `Fact` when directly observed/promoted, to `Computation` when derived.
- Statement normalization must not introduce a second truth layer — no UI-only statement cells or provider-specific blobs that bypass `Fact`/`Computation`.
- Coverage/verification state preserved via canonical value objects when source material is incomplete, conflicting, or pending promotion.
- For US issuers, SEC EDGAR is the primary-source anchor (`data.sec.gov` unauthenticated JSON APIs).
- Non-custom XBRL company facts aggregates do NOT cover segment data, issuer-specific extension facts, or non-GAAP logic — those need separate extraction (P6.1 refines).

## Depends on

- P0.3b (issuer-appropriate subject handoff)
- P0.5 (schema — `metrics`, `facts`, `computations`)

## Downstream consumers

- P1.2b (stats/segment/consensus aggregation)
- P1.3 (financials + earnings tabs)
- P2.2 (pre-resolve router — fundamentals cost class)
- P3.5 (promotion rules — normalized statement values land as Fact/Computation)
- P6.2 (non-US expansion extends accounting mappings)

## Considerations / gotchas

- `data.sec.gov`'s companyfacts is NOT segment-aware. Plan extraction infrastructure for P6.1.
- Restatements are supersessions, not overwrites — use `facts.supersedes` / `superseded_by`.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/fundamentals/StatementService.ts`
- `services/fundamentals/MetricMapper.ts`
- `services/fundamentals/sec/*`
- `/v1/fundamentals/statements` handler
- `/v1/fundamentals/profile` handler

title: Implement statement normalization (income/balance/cashflow)
description: What: Canonical normalization of the three core statement families. Why: Every consumer must get the same shape. Contract: Basis (as_reported/as_restated) is explicit; no silent merging. Verification: Test AAPL income statement against known filing.

title: Implement fiscal calendar normalization
description: What: Map issuer-specific fiscal calendars to canonical period labels. Why: FY for Apple ends in September; comparing to calendar year without normalization is a bug. Contract: Periods tagged with fiscal_year/fiscal_period and period_end. Verification: Apple FY25 and calendar 2025 are distinguishable.

title: Implement metric mapper (statement lines → Metric ids)
description: What: Mapping from normalized line items to canonical Metric definitions. Why: Displayed values must resolve to Fact/Computation rows keyed by metric_id. Contract: Every normalized line maps to exactly one metric_id. Verification: Known statement produces expected metric_ids.

title: Implement SEC EDGAR primary-source anchor
description: What: data.sec.gov JSON API integration for US issuer companyfacts. Why: Primary-source anchor for US GAAP values. Contract: companyfacts responses normalize to Facts with source_id pointing to the filing. Verification: Pull AAPL companyfacts; verify Facts created with SEC Source.

title: P1.2b Stats, segment, and consensus aggregations
description:
# P1.2b — Stats, segment, and consensus aggregations

## What

The fundamentals aggregation layer above normalized statement facts that produces reusable read models for key stats, segment facts, analyst consensus, and comparison-ready derived outputs. Aggregations expose their derivation inputs, freshness, and coverage assumptions explicitly.

## Why

Without a shared aggregation layer, every UI surface builds ad-hoc ratio/target logic from raw statements, duplicating work and drifting from canonical values. Keeping aggregations derivation-transparent preserves I1 — the consumer can always trace a displayed aggregate back to the inputs that produced it.

## Contract / acceptance

- Aggregation outputs are service-level views, not replacements for `Fact` or `Computation` rows.
- Key stats and derived ratios may combine fundamentals, market context, and deterministic computations — must expose basis, period, and `as_of` assumptions.
- Segment facts remain distinct from consolidated statement outputs: carry `axis`, `segment_definitions`, period context, `coverage_warnings`. Do not flatten segment disclosures into issuer-level statement tables.
- Analyst consensus remains distinct from both reported statements and promoted evidence facts — rating distributions, price-target summaries, analyst counts, coverage warnings with explicit `as_of`.
- Comparison-ready derived outputs may package reusable aggregate slices for peer views — must not become opaque caches of UI-specific payloads.
- Incomplete, stale, or inconsistent inputs surface warnings + partial-coverage metadata — never fabricate complete comparisons.
- May read canonical facts, computations, and provider-backed consensus/segment inputs — provenance, supersession, and truth-promotion state remain owned by the canonical value plane.

## Depends on

- P1.2 (normalized statement layer)

## Downstream consumers

- P1.3 (overview, financials, earnings reuse these read models)
- P2.2 (router classifies heavier fundamentals requests)
- P4.6 (specialized blocks cite stable aggregate envelopes)
- P6.1 (segment extraction refinement extends segment aggregates)
- P6.2 (non-US expansion — aggregates must keep basis/freshness explicit)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Analyst consensus is NOT an observed fact about the issuer — it's an aggregate of analyst opinion. Keep the distinction in the block and in labels.
- Peer comparison aggregates can be heavy — consider caching at this level (cache identity: peer-set × metric × basis × period).

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/fundamentals/aggregations/*`
- `/v1/fundamentals/stats` handler
- `/v1/fundamentals/segments` handler
- `/v1/fundamentals/consensus` handler

title: Implement key stats aggregation
description: What: Derived ratios (margins, growth, multiples) with explicit basis/period/as_of. Why: UI consumers need stable aggregate envelopes. Contract: Every stat exposes its derivation assumptions. Verification: P/E for AAPL resolves to Fact (price) × Computation (ratio) with visible inputs.

title: Implement segment-fact aggregation (axis + definitions + coverage_warnings)
description: What: Segment views distinct from consolidated statements, with axis/definitions/warnings. Why: Flattening segments into issuer-level tables loses disclosure structure. Contract: Response carries axis (business/geography), definitions, coverage_warnings. Verification: Apple Services vs Products segment results preserved separately.

title: Implement analyst consensus aggregation
description: What: Rating distribution, price targets, analyst counts, coverage warnings with explicit as_of. Why: Consensus is aggregate of opinion, NOT observed fact. Contract: Consensus is tagged as an aggregate, distinct from reported Fact. Verification: UI can distinguish 'consensus EPS estimate' from 'reported EPS'.

title: P1.3 Symbol detail (overview, financials, earnings core tabs)
description:
# P1.3 — Symbol detail (overview, financials, earnings core tabs)

## What

Symbol detail's three core deterministic sections: `overview` (extends the thin quote landing into a durable core tab with profile + key stats + limited performance context), `financials` (normalized statement tables, statement-linked trend views, segment-aware breakdowns), `earnings` (deterministic earnings chronology, expectation-vs-result views, consensus summaries).

## Why

These three tabs are the deterministic core of the symbol workspace — the foundation that Analyze (P4.2), Home deep-links (P4.4), and chat pivots all rely on. Fixing their ownership boundaries here prevents later surfaces from collapsing them into one scrolling page or duplicating ownership.

## Contract / acceptance

- `overview` composes listing-aware quote context, company profile, key stats, limited performance/context summary. Does NOT become a second home for full statement tables, holders, or interpretive evidence flows.
- `financials` owns normalized statement tables + statement-linked trends + segment-aware breakdowns. Composes normalized statements + aggregation layer for key stats + segment facts. Does NOT rebuild fundamentals logic from provider-specific payloads.
- `earnings` owns earnings chronology + expectation-vs-result + consensus summaries. Composes earnings-release events + EPS surprise + consensus + price-target context. Does NOT turn transcript reading, news clustering, or freeform commentary into tab-owned responsibilities.
- All three tabs live inside the subject-detail shell and share subject header context + nested-route navigation.
- Core tab composition depends on hydrated subject identity, market quote/series services, fundamentals profile/statement services, aggregation outputs, and structured earnings events — through backend contracts, not direct provider payloads or chat-style tool loops.
- Moving between overview/financials/earnings preserves subject context and shell chrome — local section transition, not a new top-level workspace.
- Tabs may link between each other through stable section destinations. Must not collapse into one scrolling page.

## Depends on

- P0.4 (landing state to extend)
- P1.1 + P1.1b (market data + series)
- P1.2 + P1.2b (fundamentals + aggregations)

## Downstream consumers

- P1.3b (holders, signals, Analyze entry layered on top)
- P4.2 (Analyze launches from symbol detail preserving subject context)
- P4.4 (Home deep-links into the right deterministic tab)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Do NOT use chat-style tool loops inside symbol tabs. These are deterministic surfaces.
- Earnings tab must distinguish reported earnings (Fact) from consensus/estimate (aggregate).

## Files to touch (likely)

- `app/symbol/overview/*`
- `app/symbol/financials/*`
- `app/symbol/earnings/*`

title: Implement overview tab (quote + profile + key stats + limited performance)
description: What: Durable overview composing listing quote + profile + key stats + limited performance/context summary. Why: Extends the thin landing into a real core tab. Contract: Does NOT duplicate financials or holders ownership. Verification: Overview tab shows price, company blurb, key stats row, small perf chart.

title: Implement financials tab (statement tables + trend views + segment breakdowns)
description: What: Normalized statement tables with statement-linked trend visualizations and segment-aware breakdowns. Why: The main financial surface for the symbol. Contract: Basis toggle (as_reported vs as_restated) is explicit. Verification: Income statement with last 5 fiscal years + segment donut + trajectory.

title: Implement earnings tab (chronology + EPS surprise + consensus)
description: What: Earnings-release events, EPS surprise history, analyst consensus, price-target context. Why: Deterministic earnings chronology surface. Contract: Does NOT include transcript reading or freeform commentary (those belong to chat/signals). Verification: Earnings tab shows last 8 quarters estimate vs actual + consensus + price target range.

title: P1.3b Symbol detail (holders, signals, Analyze entry integration)
description:
# P1.3b — Symbol detail (holders, signals, Analyze entry integration)

## What

The adjacent symbol-detail sections and launch points layered onto P1.3's core: `holders` (institutional + insider holder views), `signals` (community views, news pulse, future alt-data — extensible route bucket), plus explicit handoff from symbol detail into top-level `Analyze` with carried `SubjectRef` context.

## Why

Holders, signals, and Analyze entry are first-class symbol-detail affordances in the videos. Keeping them distinct from the core tabs (overview/financials/earnings) preserves those tabs' deterministic ownership and prevents scope creep.

## Contract / acceptance

- `holders` composes structured holder outputs from the fundamentals service. Remains distinct from portfolio overlays, watchlist state, and user-owned monitoring.
- `signals` is the symbol-detail section for Reddit-like community views, news pulse, future alt-data. This bead does NOT replace that route bucket with a source-specific `/reddit` shell.
- Reddit-like/news-specialized content inside `signals` composes evidence-backed blocks, claim clusters, evidence bundles, and trend-style renderers — not raw social-text panes or provider-specific mini-pages.
- `Analyze` remains a top-level workspace, NOT a durable nested symbol-detail section.
- Symbol detail launches `Analyze` with carried `SubjectRef` context or an explicit analyze intent. Resulting artifacts live on the shared snapshot + block plane, not inside a persistent symbol-detail tab.
- Entering `holders` or `signals` preserves subject context inside the same nested-route family and subject-detail shell.
- Launching `Analyze` from symbol detail is a workspace transition that still preserves carried subject context (no reinterpretation from raw ticker text).
- Handoff into `Analyze` stays explicit so later shared-artifact/replay flows point to durable snapshot-backed artifacts (not scraped UI state).

## Depends on

- P1.3 (core tabs)

## Downstream consumers

- P4.2 (Analyze template system — receives carried subject context from this handoff)
- P4.3 (shared artifact flow — consumes sealed artifacts produced by Analyze entry)
- P4.6 (specialized social/news blocks — live under `signals` and reuse evidence-backed blocks)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Naming discipline: `signals` NOT `reddit` even if the current vendor content is mostly Reddit. The route bucket must survive vendor swaps.
- Analyze launches from symbol detail carry the `SubjectRef`, not a ticker string.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `app/symbol/holders/*`
- `app/symbol/signals/*`
- `app/analyze/entry.ts` (handoff)

title: Implement holders tab (institutional + insider)
description: What: Structured holder views from fundamentals service. Why: Distinct from portfolio overlays and watchlist state. Contract: Holder tab consumes get_holders; no scraped text. Verification: AAPL holders shows top institutional + recent insider transactions.

title: Implement signals tab (extensible community/sentiment/alt-data bucket)
description: What: Route bucket for Reddit-like community views, news pulse, future alt-data. Why: Source-agnostic naming survives vendor swaps. Contract: Content composes evidence-backed blocks (claim clusters, trend renderers), not raw social panes. Verification: Signals tab shows a sentiment trend + recent claim clusters.

title: Implement Analyze entry handoff from symbol detail
description: What: Explicit handoff from symbol detail into top-level Analyze with carried SubjectRef context. Why: Preserves canonical identity into Analyze; prevents re-resolution from raw text. Contract: Analyze receives SubjectRef + optional analyze intent. Verification: Click 'Analyze' on AAPL detail; Analyze workspace loads with AAPL context prefilled.

title: P1.4 Screener query model & result contract
description:
# P1.4 — Screener query model & result contract

## What

The service-owned screener query surface: structured filter-and-rank envelopes (not a freeform DSL), bound to screener-owned fields backed by market-data and fundamentals services. Defines the result-row shape and the persisted `screen` subject (replayable query definition + ordering semantics).

## Why

Screener consumers (UI, dynamic watchlists, agents, theme derivation) must see one query envelope shape. Allowing arbitrary provider-payload joins or client-side DSLs multiplies bug surface and breaks the single-source principle.

## Contract / acceptance

- Query envelopes are explicit structured filter-and-rank, not freeform DSL or user-authored formulas.
- Minimum query dimensions: universe constraints, market/quote constraints, fundamentals/aggregate constraints, sort spec, page/limit.
- Query clauses bind to screener-owned fields backed by market-data and fundamentals services — no raw provider payload columns, no frontend-computed joins.
- Screener response is an ordered derived result set — not a new canonical identity type.
- Each result row carries canonical market subject identity, display identity, ranking/sort context, and compact quote/fundamentals summaries sufficient for screener-table rendering.
- Screener rows are thinner than symbol-detail hydration: selecting a row hands off canonical subject identity for symbol-entry flow. Does NOT embed a full symbol workspace payload.
- Reusable `screen` subject represents the persisted query definition + ordering semantics — NOT a frozen list of prehydrated row payloads.
- Service owns: query validation, execution, ranking, pagination, row-envelope assembly.
- `/v1/screener/*` is the client boundary. Clients must NOT fan out to `/v1/market/*` + `/v1/fundamentals/*` and invent join semantics.

## Depends on

- P1.1, P1.1b (market data)
- P1.2, P1.2b (fundamentals)

## Downstream consumers

- P1.4b (screener surface flow + saved-screen handoff)
- P4.1 (themes — screen-to-theme handoff)
- P4.7 (dynamic watchlists — replay screen definition)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Saving a screen persists the query definition, not a snapshot of rows. Rehydrating reruns the query — this is the whole point of replayability.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/screener/QueryService.ts`
- `services/screener/Ranker.ts`
- `/v1/screener/*` handlers

title: Implement screener query envelope + validator
description: What: Structured filter-and-rank envelope with universe/market/fundamentals/sort/limit dimensions. Why: Single shape every screener consumer consumes. Contract: No freeform DSL; no raw provider columns. Verification: Valid envelope executes; invalid rejected.

title: Implement screener result-row envelope
description: What: Result rows carry canonical subject identity + display + rank context + compact quote/fundamentals summaries. Why: Row is thinner than symbol-detail hydration. Contract: Selecting a row hands off SubjectRef for symbol-entry flow. Verification: Row selection navigates to /symbol/:subjectRef.

title: Implement persistable screen subject + replay
description: What: screen SubjectRef representing persisted query definition + ordering. Why: Replayable source for dynamic watchlists + themes. Contract: Reopen runs the query; does NOT replay stale rows. Verification: Save + reopen yields fresh execution.

title: P1.4b Screener surface flow & saved-screen handoff
description:
# P1.4b — Screener surface flow & saved-screen handoff

## What

The screener UI workspace and the session-gated saved-screen lifecycle. Screener is a primary workspace for building, refining, and viewing one active screen definition plus its current result set inside the persistent shell.

## Why

Splitting query editing, results, and saved screens into separate surfaces would force the user to context-switch for every refinement. Unifying them inside one screener workspace matches the videos' flow.

## Contract / acceptance

- Screener keeps query controls and result rows in one workspace flow. No separate "query editor" or "results viewer" primary surfaces.
- Public screener browsing may execute and refine unsaved screens without session (uses the public-route contract).
- Saving a screen / opening user-owned saved screen / mutating saved-screen metadata requires a session and uses the inline auth interrupt from P0.1.
- Saving persists the replayable screen definition + user-owned screen record + ordering semantics — NOT a frozen cache of row payloads.
- Reopening a saved screen restores the workspace with saved-screen definition as active query context; rehydrates results through the screener service (no replaying stale table rows from client storage).
- Selecting a result row hands off canonical subject identity into the existing symbol-detail entry flow.
- Handoffs into later list or theme workflows carry explicit `screen` source reference so downstream derivation can explain where a universe came from.
- This bead does NOT define dynamic watchlist derivation, theme membership inference, or bulk portfolio-overlay behavior (those belong to P4.7).

## Depends on

- P1.4 (query model)
- P0.1 (auth interrupt)
- P0.4 (symbol-entry flow — row selection)

## Downstream consumers

- P4.1 (themes — screen-to-theme handoff)
- P4.7 (dynamic watchlists — saved screen as replayable source)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Row selection is handoff of `SubjectRef`, not embed of a workspace. Don't render a full symbol workspace inline.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `app/screener/ScreenerWorkspace.tsx`
- `app/screener/SavedScreens.tsx`

title: Implement screener workspace (query controls + rows unified)
description: What: One workspace for editing + running screens. Why: Splitting into separate surfaces forces context switches. Contract: Refinements rerun the query in place. Verification: Edit a filter; results update without route change.

title: Implement saved-screen CRUD with session gate
description: What: Saving/opening/mutating saved screens requires session via inline auth interrupt. Why: Saved screens are user-owned. Contract: Public screener browsing needs no session; save prompts. Verification: Unauth save triggers auth gate preserving current screen definition.

title: P1.5 Portfolio holdings model & overlay inputs
description:
# P1.5 — Portfolio holdings model & overlay inputs

## What
