Lightweight portfolio research context (not brokerage execution): tracks held exposure for overlays and monitoring. A `Portfolio` owns one explicit `base_currency` interpreting cost assumptions and overlay totals. `PortfolioHolding` binds to canonical market identity (typically `instrument` or `listing`), plus quantity, optional cost basis, and open/close timestamps.

## Why

Portfolio overlays need a defined input contract. Without explicit `base_currency` and canonical-identity binding, overlay math sprays implicit FX assumptions and ticker-string persistence across surfaces.

## Contract / acceptance

- Portfolio support is lightweight research context: tracks held exposure for overlays and monitoring — NOT brokerage execution, order management, tax lots, cash ledgers, settlement.
- `Portfolio.base_currency` is required and defines reporting currency for holding cost assumptions and overlay totals.
- `base_currency` is a reporting/comparison assumption, not proof that the underlying listing trades in that currency, and not a full FX accounting model.
- `PortfolioHolding` binds to canonical market identity (`instrument` or `listing`) — must NOT persist raw ticker strings or higher-order subjects (theme, macro_topic, portfolio, screen) as holding identity.
- `cost_basis` is optional; when present, interpreted in the portfolio's `base_currency`. No separate transaction currencies, FX lots, or fee-adjusted basis accounting.
- Overlay inputs from holdings are read models keyed by subject + contributing portfolio — not new canonical subject identities or stored UI payloads.
- Minimum overlay input contract: held-state, contributing `portfolio_id`, quantity, optional cost basis, base-currency label.
- Multiple portfolios holding the same subject with different base currencies: contributions stay distinct (no silent netting through implicit FX).
- Manual watchlists and holdings are separate: saving a subject does not create a holding; holding a subject does not add it to a watchlist.

## Depends on

- P0.4b (manual watchlist baseline)
- P0.4 (subject entry)

## Downstream consumers

- P1.5b (first surface behaviors)
- P4.7 (dynamic watchlists + portfolio overlays — consume the overlay input contract)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Do NOT model margin, realized tax, fees, or order history. This is research, not brokerage.
- Use `subject_kind + subject_id` polymorphism; enforce allowed kinds (instrument, listing) in application code.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/portfolio/HoldingsService.ts`
- `/v1/portfolios/*` handlers

title: Implement Portfolio model with base_currency
description: What: Portfolio with required base_currency + holdings CRUD. Why: Overlay math needs an explicit reporting currency. Contract: base_currency is required at create time. Verification: Create portfolio without base_currency fails.

title: Implement PortfolioHolding binding to canonical market identity
description: What: Holdings bind to instrument or listing SubjectRef (NOT ticker, NOT higher-order subjects). Why: Identity discipline + overlay correctness. Contract: Invalid subject_kinds rejected. Verification: Attempt to hold a theme or screen fails.

title: Implement overlay input read model (subject × contributing portfolio × quantity × cost basis)
description: What: Read model for overlay inputs keyed by subject + portfolio. Why: Overlay is not a new subject kind; it's derived. Contract: Multiple portfolios hold same subject with different currencies stay distinct. Verification: Two portfolios holding AAPL (USD, EUR) contribute separately.

title: Implement entitlement_channels egress gate
description: What: Export/share respects entitlement_channels. Why: License compliance. Contract: app-only facts cannot egress via export/email. Verification: Attempt export of app-only fact; blocked.

title: Persist chat_messages only after snapshot.sealed
description: What: Message persistence is gated on seal success. Why: Persisting before seal means invariant-violating messages become history. Contract: No chat_message row without snapshot_id. Verification: Induce verifier failure; confirm no message row.

title: Implement manifest staging from tool-call log
description: What: Assemble manifest from tool-call outputs. Why: Manifest content must trace to actual tool calls. Contract: No fact in manifest lacks a tool_call_id. Verification: Audit staged manifest against tool log.

title: Implement analyze_template_run bundle execution
description: What: Bundle execution produces a sealed snapshot + memo Block[]. Why: Memos are snapshot-backed artifacts. Contract: Each run produces new snapshot; old memos remain readable at their snapshots. Verification: Run template twice; both memos readable with distinct snapshots.

title: Implement agent cadence compiler (hourly/daily/on-filing)
description: What: Human-readable cadence strings -> schedule. Why: Users don't write cron. Contract: Unsupported cadence rejected. Verification: Parse golden set.

title: Implement allowed_transforms legality checker
description: What: Transform endpoint consults manifest; legal transforms serve, illegal get 409. Why: Interactivity inside snapshot vs refresh is the hard boundary. Contract: Range-change within as_of is 200; basis-change is 409. Verification: Contract tests for both paths.

title: Implement XBRL extension + segment extractor
description: What: Extractor for segment data + issuer-specific extensions. Why: companyfacts excludes them. Contract: Segment rows preserve axis + definition_as_of. Verification: AAPL segments extracted correctly.

title: Implement sealed-snapshot handoff between surfaces
description: What: Add-to-chat references the same snapshot_id as the source memo/finding. Why: Prevents re-run inconsistencies. Contract: Added artifact block carries origin snapshot_id. Verification: Transforms on added block hit the same snapshot endpoint.

title: Implement approval interceptor for write-intent tools
description: What: Tools with approval_required: true return pending_action_id. Why: I7 enforcement. Contract: create_alert and create_agent both route through interceptor. Verification: Contract tests for both.

title: Implement bundle selector driven by pre-resolve classification
description: What: Bundle selection from 11 bundles. Why: System chooses bundle; model chooses tools. Contract: Unknown bundle fails turn gracefully. Verification: Unit tests across bundle names.

title: Implement per-thread coordinator serializing turn state
description: What: One coordinator per thread_id serializing tool calls + sealing + SSE emission. Why: Prevents race conditions across overlapping turns. Contract: Two concurrent user messages on same thread queue deterministically. Verification: Load test with burst of messages.

title: Implement RunActivity emitter with SSE stream
description: What: Stage events emitted during agent runs; SSE-delivered to right-rail. Why: Live visibility into agent execution. Contract: Every stage transition emits; persists to table. Verification: Trigger agent run; see stream fill.

title: Implement alert evaluation + firing log
description: What: Evaluate per run; log firings. Why: Auditability. Contract: Firing log names the triggering row(s). Verification: Inspect log after known trigger.

title: Implement digest batching + throttling
description: What: Batch multiple findings per channel; throttle bursts. Why: Avoid notification spam. Contract: Per-user per-channel rate caps. Verification: Burst test produces one batched notification.

title: Implement prompt-cache prefix order (tools->system->bundle->schema->fewshots->summary->subject)
description: What: Enforce cache-stable prefix ordering; user turn is last. Why: Anthropic caching has 5-min TTL; wrong order evicts cache. Contract: Prefix hashes stable across turns within a bundle. Verification: Cache-hit rate ≥ threshold.

title: Implement drift report (compare consecutive eval runs)
description: What: Drift detector between runs flags regressions. Why: Drift detection is the point of evals. Contract: Report lists regressed categories with deltas. Verification: Synthetic regression triggers alert.

title: Implement create_alert approval flow
description: What: create_alert returns pending_action_id. Why: Alerts egress. Contract: No delivery until approved. Verification: Create alert; no notifications until approve.

title: Epic PX — Cross-cutting (observability, tool runtime, frontend)
description:
# Epic PX — Cross-cutting

## Goal

Infrastructure that spans phases and cannot live inside one of them: observability and evals, the tool runtime (bundle selection, audience enforcement, approvals, budgets), and the frontend BlockRegistry renderer. These have to exist by the time P2 comes online, but they evolve through every phase.

## Child tasks

- PX.1 Observability — `tool_call_log`, `citation_log`, `verifier_fail_log`, `extraction_eval`, golden-set evals, drift report
- PX.2 Tool runtime & orchestration — registry loader, bundle selector, audience enforcement, cost-class budgets, approval interceptors, prompt-cache ordering
- PX.3 Frontend renderer — `BlockRegistry` keyed by block `kind`, streaming, virtualized lists, scroll tailing, skeleton-first charts, memoized blocks by content hash

## Exit criteria

- Every assistant turn produces `tool_call_log`, `citation_log`, and (if sealed) a snapshot row.
- `finance_research_tool_registry.json` is the single source of truth for tool metadata.
- Audience enforcement is automated: analyst-audience tools cannot receive raw document bodies under any prompt sequence.
- A block rendered in chat, Analyze, and a finding uses the same React component instance from the registry.
- Prompt-cache order is `tools → system → bundle policy → response schema → few-shots → thread summary → resolved subjects/period → user turn`.

## Invariants this epic primarily guards

- **I4** — PX.2 is the code path that enforces "analyst never sees raw text."
- **I7** — PX.2 is the code path that intercepts write-intent tools into pending actions.

## Why cross-cutting

PX.1 observability is needed by every other epic (without it, evals can't catch regressions). PX.2 tool runtime is the engine inside P2's turn loop and P5's agent loop. PX.3 renderer is consumed by chat (P2.3), Home (P4.4), Analyze (P4.2), and findings (P5.3).

title: PX.1 Observability (tool_call_log, citation_log, verifier_fail, evals)
description:
# PX.1 — Observability (tool_call_log, citation_log, verifier_fail, evals)

## What

The observability and evaluation surface: structured logging (`tool_call_logs`, `citation_logs`, `verifier_fail_logs`), extraction eval metrics, golden-set evaluation runs, drift report generation.

## Why

Without observability, invariant regressions are invisible until users notice. The spec names these log tables as day-one essentials — not an afterthought — because evals are how we catch drift before it ships.

## Contract / acceptance

- Every tool invocation writes `tool_call_logs` with duration, status, error_code.
- Every block citation writes `citation_logs` bound to `snapshot_id`.
- Verifier failures write `verifier_fail_logs` with `reason_code` and `details`.
- Extraction evals run nightly against a golden set; results land in `eval_run_results`.
- Golden set categories (from spec): ticker/name disambiguation; issuer-vs-listing confusion; themes and macro topics; fiscal/calendar alignment; corporate actions; restatements; segment redefinitions; low analyst coverage; delisted/acquired names; multi-entity documents; candidate-vs-authoritative fact promotion; social rumor handling; block-choice correctness; snapshot transform correctness.
- Drift report flags regressions between consecutive eval runs.

## Depends on

- P0.6 (log tables bootstrapped)

## Downstream consumers

- All phases (observability is consumed universally).

## Considerations / gotchas

- Eval runs are not free. Schedule nightly, not per-PR.
- Track `model_version` and `prompt_version` so eval results are attributable.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `observability/logger/*`
- `evals/golden/*`
- `evals/runner.ts`

title: Implement agent_run_log operational audit writer
description: What: Operational log distinct from RunActivity — captures start/end, inputs watermark, outputs summary, errors, duration per agent run. Why: RunActivity is user-facing telemetry; agent_run_log is ops audit (per spec §20). Contract: Row per run; indexed by agent_id + start_ts. Verification: Run agent; row appears with correct duration + outcome.

title: PX.2 Tool runtime & orchestration
description:
# PX.2 — Tool runtime & orchestration

## What

The runtime that loads `spec/finance_research_tool_registry.json`, selects tool bundles, enforces audience separation (reader vs analyst), gates tools by cost class, intercepts write-intent tools into pending-action approvals, and maintains the prompt-cache prefix order.

## Why

This is the code path that physically enforces I4 (analyst never sees raw untrusted text) and I7 (approvals). Without it, the invariants exist only in documentation.

## Contract / acceptance

- Registry loader reads `finance_research_tool_registry.json` as the source of truth for bundle membership, audience, `read_only`, `approval_required`, `cost_class`, `freshness_expectation`.
- Bundle selector chooses from the 11 bundles based on pre-resolve classification (P2.2).
- Audience enforcement is **static** where possible (typed tool interfaces distinguish reader vs analyst) and **runtime** where necessary (e.g., reject analyst call with raw-document argument).
- Budget gate limits tool-call count per cost class per turn.
- Write-intent tools (`create_alert`, `create_agent`, `add_to_watchlist`) are intercepted: `approval_required: true` → pending action awaiting user confirmation; `approval_required: false` → may execute but remains a policy boundary.
- Prompt-cache prefix order: `tools → system → bundle policy → response schema → few-shots → thread summary → resolved subjects/period` — user turn is last.

## Depends on

- P0.6 (observability tables for tool_call_logs)

## Downstream consumers

- P2.2 (pre-resolve router consumes bundle selector)
- P2.4 (verifier consults approval states)
- P3.2 (reader-audience tools are enforced here)
- P5.1, P5.4 (approval-sensitive tool flows)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Test audience enforcement with adversarial prompts. An analyst-audience tool accepting a raw_blob_url string is a regression.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/tools/Registry.ts`
- `services/tools/Orchestrator.ts`
- `services/tools/AudienceEnforcer.ts`
- `services/tools/ApprovalInterceptor.ts`

title: Implement analyst prompt templates per bundle
description: What: Per-bundle system + policy prompts wired into the prompt-cache prefix. Why: Each bundle (quote_lookup, theme_research, etc.) has different response expectations; generic prompts waste tokens. Contract: Bundle selection in P2.2 pulls the right template. Verification: 11 templates present; bundle selector maps each bundle to one.

title: PX.3 Frontend renderer & BlockRegistry
description:
# PX.3 — Frontend renderer & BlockRegistry

## What

The React-side rendering layer: `BlockRegistry` keyed by block `kind`, streaming block rendering driven by SSE events, virtualized message list for long threads, memoized blocks by content hash, skeleton-first chart rendering, scroll tailing with jump-to-latest.

## Why

Block rendering is the user-visible surface. Performance cliffs here (long threads re-render, charts flash, scroll jumps) kill the feel of the videos.

## Contract / acceptance

- `BlockRegistry` is the single source for `kind → Component` mapping; chat, Analyze, and findings share it.
- SSE-driven incremental rendering: `block.began` creates a skeleton; `block.delta` updates; `block.completed` finalizes.
- Virtualized message list in long threads (windowing).
- Blocks memoized by content hash so unchanged blocks never re-render.
- Charts render skeleton first, data after.
- Scroll tails new messages; jump-to-latest affordance when user has scrolled up.
- Chart blocks fetch data via backend (`snapshot_id + transform`) — NEVER direct vendor calls.
- Interactivity stays inside snapshot scope unless user explicitly refreshes.

## Depends on

- P2.1 (SSE transport)
- P2.3 (block registry + renderers)
- P2.4 (snapshot transform endpoint)

## Downstream consumers

- (leaf — user-visible)

## Considerations / gotchas

- Frontend renderer must never treat rendering as a tool invocation. Output is typed data; rendering is typed UI.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `app/chat/ChatView.tsx`
- `app/chat/MessageList.tsx` (virtualized)
- `app/blocks/Registry.ts`

title: PX.4 Title & summary async model (thread titles, finding summaries, Home headlines)
description:
# PX.4 — Title & summary async model

## What

An async model pass that generates: chat thread titles, thread summaries (rolling compression for the prompt-cache prefix), finding summaries, and Home card headlines. This is the fifth model role called out by the spec (`§12.5 Summary/title model`).

## Why

Without this role, chats use "New chat" forever, thread summaries don't compress, Home cards show raw cluster text, and finding headlines are claim-text dumps. The videos show clean "Iran-US Trade Tensions" style thread titles and concise finding headlines — those are generated, not user-typed.

## Contract / acceptance

- Thread title generator runs async after first exchange; updates `chat_threads.title`.
- Thread summary rolls per N turns, replacing older turns in the cache prefix.
- Finding summary generator produces `findings.headline` from sealed snapshot + cluster.
- Home card headline generator produces one-line headline from finding + cluster context.
- All outputs validate against a title/summary schema (no blocks — these are plain text by spec).
- Cheap/fast model class (this is explicitly NOT the analyst).
- Failures fall back to raw first-N-chars rather than blocking the surface.

## Depends on

- P2.1 (thread coordinator — writes titles)
- P5.3 (findings — generates finding summaries)
- P4.4 (Home — consumes headlines)

## Downstream consumers

- Every user-facing title/headline surface.

## Considerations / gotchas

- Async failures must never block the user-facing turn. Title stays as placeholder until generation succeeds.
- Rolling summaries must be deterministic enough that the prompt-cache prefix stays stable — don't summarize on every turn.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `services/summary/TitleGenerator.ts`
- `services/summary/SummaryGenerator.ts`
- `services/summary/HeadlineGenerator.ts`

title: Implement thread title generator (async after first exchange)
description: What: Async worker that reads first assistant response + user intent and writes a concise title to chat_threads.title. Why: 'New chat' forever is the failure mode. Contract: Title is <= 60 chars; single line; async failure falls back to placeholder. Verification: Create thread; after first turn, title is human-readable.

title: Implement rolling thread summary for prompt-cache prefix
description: What: Compresses older turns into a summary that sits in the cache-stable prefix of the next prompt. Why: Long threads break the 5-min prompt-cache TTL without compression. Contract: Summary regenerates only after N turns (not every turn) so prefix stays stable. Verification: Turn 20+ uses summary; cache hit rate stays healthy.

title: Implement finding summary + Home card headline generators
description: What: Generate findings.headline + Home card headline from sealed snapshot + cluster. Why: Raw claim text is not a headline. Contract: Headlines <= 80 chars; deterministic for a given snapshot. Verification: Same snapshot produces same headline across runs.

title: Implement section-block layout hint rendering
description: What: Layout hint drives memo section ordering + nesting. Why: Memos look structured like the videos. Contract: block_layout_hint is advisory, not enforced. Verification: Two templates with different layout hints produce visibly different memos.

title: Implement citation_log writer on block binding
description: What: Every block citation writes citation_logs bound to snapshot_id. Why: Prove every displayed value traces back. Contract: Row per (block, ref). Verification: Send chat turn; rows for each ref.

title: Implement Finding generator with sealed snapshot per finding
description: What: Every finding calls P2.4 seal. Why: Reproducibility. Contract: Finding has snapshot_id pointing to sealed manifest. Verification: Finding blocks resolve refs via snapshot.

title: Implement JSON-schema validator wired to finance_research_block_schema.json
description: What: Validator that rejects any block not matching the normative schema. Why: Model output must be typed artifact, not free-form. Contract: Invalid blocks fail the turn at verification. Verification: Synthetic malformed block fails validation.

title: Implement per-source-class promotion rules
description: What: Deterministic rules mapping source_kind + confidence + corroboration -> verification_status. Why: Documents-are-evidence-not-truth discipline. Contract: Rules table documents each decision path. Verification: Golden inputs produce expected verification_status.

title: Implement private-state stripping on shared artifacts
description: What: Strip watchlist/held badges + portfolio qty from shared artifacts by default. Why: Privacy. Contract: Shared artifact renders for recipient without user-owned overlay state. Verification: Share memo; recipient does not see sharer's portfolio context.

title: Implement EntityImpact edges (direction/channel/horizon)
description: What: EntityImpact rows routing claims to subjects with direction+channel+horizon. Why: Routing, alerting, theme relevance all key off impacts. Contract: Impacts ≠ mentions. Verification: Impact channel set drives notification decisions.

title: Implement in-snapshot interactivity on added artifacts
description: What: Added artifact blocks honor allowed_transforms. Why: Cross-surface interactivity without refresh. Contract: Legal transforms work; illegal show refresh prompt. Verification: Add perf_comparison to chat; change range.

title: Implement summary_blocks generation via BlockRegistry
description: What: Findings render via the same block registry as chat/Analyze. Why: Shared renderer principle. Contract: summary_blocks validates against the block schema. Verification: Finding blocks render in Home deep-linked views.

title: Implement source-category -> tool-bundle mapping
description: What: User-facing 'source categories' map to internal tool bundles + policies. Why: Users select UX-level categories, not tool names. Contract: Mapping is declarative + auditable. Verification: Golden templates map to expected bundles.

title: Implement sentiment_trend + mention_volume block renderers
description: What: Trend-style blocks reusing P1.1b series semantics. Why: Consistent chart semantics across block kinds. Contract: Cache identity matches P1.1b rules. Verification: Render trend block; change range via allowed_transforms.

title: Implement EvidenceBundle assembler
description: What: build_evidence_bundle returning documents + evidence for claim/event ids. Why: Analyst-facing evidence without raw bodies. Contract: Analyst tool call returns metadata + locators + excerpt_hashes only. Verification: Bundle for a cluster returns all supporting docs.

title: Implement bundle selector + budget policy
description: What: System selects bundle from 11 options; budget caps tool calls per cost class. Why: Constrains token/latency costs deterministically. Contract: Budget exhaustion returns a partial-answer path + explicit note. Verification: Stress test with many tool calls.

title: Implement transactional watermark storage + advance
description: What: Watermarks advance in same txn as finding emission. Why: Partial advances corrupt delta detection. Contract: Failure reverts both. Verification: Induce mid-run failure; watermark unchanged.

title: Implement market pulse + watchlist movers + agent summaries + pinned screens
description: What: Secondary Home sections. Why: Videos show these alongside findings. Contract: Each section uses existing deterministic services; no new truth layer. Verification: Home page renders all sections with live data.

title: Implement subject extraction via resolver envelopes
description: What: Pre-resolve uses P0.3 resolver; ambiguity surfaces as a clarification response. Why: Never let the model silently resolve identity. Contract: Ambiguous inputs produce a clarification turn. Verification: User says 'GOOG' — system asks which share class when ambiguous.

title: Implement alert rule compiler (declarative -> evaluable predicates)
description: What: Compile declarative rules. Why: Rules must be reviewable and replayable. Contract: Rule definition is JSON-serializable. Verification: Compile + evaluate canonical rules.

title: Implement Agent model with thesis/universe/cadence/alert_rules
description: What: Agent row with all config. Why: Durable agent definition. Contract: Universe may be static SubjectRef[] or dynamic (screen/theme/portfolio). Verification: Create agents with both universe modes.

title: Implement RichText ref resolver (fact/claim/event lookup in snapshot)
description: What: RichText segments with ref_kind + ref_id resolve from the snapshot manifest at render time. Why: Narrative values come from the same source as chart values. Contract: A missing ref renders a visible error, not silent omission. Verification: Test with missing fact id.

title: Implement SSE event schema + sequence numbers
description: What: Normative event set with monotonically increasing sequence numbers and turn/block correlation. Why: Frontend reconnect must resume from last delivered sequence. Contract: All 10 event kinds carry turn_id + seq. Verification: Kill+resume SSE; last-seq reconnect completes stream.

title: Implement inferred theme membership from claims + impacts
description: What: Membership inferred from ClaimCluster + EntityImpact signals with rationale_claim_ids. Why: Explainability of inferred membership. Contract: Every inferred member has rationale pointing to source claims. Verification: Inspect rationale chain.

title: Implement reader-audience tool wiring (extract_*, classify_sentiment)
description: What: Reader tools wired to registry; enforce raw-text-only-in-reader-audience. Why: Prompt-injection containment frontier. Contract: Analyst-audience tools cannot be called on raw document bytes. Verification: Static audit + runtime reject.

title: Implement non-GAAP reconciliation extractor
description: What: Extract non-GAAP reconciliations with explicit GAAP mapping. Why: Investors care about both. Contract: Each non-GAAP value links to GAAP counterpart. Verification: Reconciliation table renders with both sides.

title: Implement cache hit-rate + cache-identity audit dashboards
description: What: Monitoring for P1.1b cache effectiveness. Why: Optimize hit rate without weakening correctness. Contract: Dashboard shows hit rate by cache-identity dim. Verification: Dashboard populated in dev.

title: Route low-confidence extractions to reviewer queue
description: What: Below-threshold extractions enter reviewer queue instead of auto-promoting. Why: Silent promotion of bad extractions is an I3 violation. Contract: Threshold configurable; queue feeds P6.3. Verification: Simulated low-confidence extraction lands in queue.

title: Implement Fact supersession (no overwrites)
description: What: Restatements use facts.supersedes/superseded_by. Why: Overwriting loses audit trail. Contract: Original fact row never modified. Verification: Restated filing produces new fact; old row unchanged.

title: Implement registry loader reading finance_research_tool_registry.json
description: What: Load normative registry. Why: Single source of truth. Contract: Changes to JSON reflect in runtime without code changes. Verification: Edit registry; restart; new tool visible.

title: Implement news_cluster block renderer
description: What: Block renderer bound to ClaimCluster + supporting docs. Why: Shows dedupe + sources. Contract: claim_refs + document_refs required. Verification: Cluster of three news items renders as one news_cluster block.
